note: Per-symbol description timeline across archived Claude Code binaries: the command/skill registry description captured per version, collapsed to change points (eras). Each era holds from the given version until the next. Lets a snapshot carry the description the symbol actually had at that version instead of one current string. Generated by scripts/backfill-binary.ts; consumed by scripts/scrape-changelog.ts.

cli_flag:--ablation.description: Run a no-plugin baseline arm and report the score delta (none | with-without; default: with-without when targeting a plugin by name (installed or skills-dir), none for a path)

cli_flag:--add-dir.description: Additional directories to allow tool access to (must be absolute paths)
Additional directories to allow tool access to

cli_flag:--advisor.description: Enable the server-side advisor tool with the specified model (alias or full ID).

cli_flag:--agent.description: Agent for the current session. Overrides the 'agent' setting.

cli_flag:--agent-type.description: Custom agent type for this teammate

cli_flag:--agents.description: JSON object defining custom agents (e.g. '{"reviewer": {"description": "Reviews code", "prompt": "You are a code reviewer"}}')

cli_flag:--allow-dangerously-skip-permis.description: Enable bypassing all permission checks as an option, without it being enabled by default. Recommended only for sandboxes with no internet access.

cli_flag:--allow-tools.description: Operator grant for gated tools (Bash, Write, Edit, WebFetch, mcp__*). Supports Tool(pattern:*) syntax

cli_flag:--allowed.description: Comma-separated list of tool names to allow (e.g. "Bash(git*),Edit,Replace")
Comma or space-separated list of tool names to allow (e.g. "Bash(git*) Edit Replace")
Comma or space-separated list of tool names to allow (e.g. "Bash(git*) Edit Write")
Comma or space-separated list of tool names to allow (e.g. "Bash(git:*) Edit Write")
Comma or space-separated list of tool names to allow (e.g. "Bash(git:*) Edit")
Comma or space-separated list of tool names to allow (e.g. "Bash(git *) Edit")

cli_flag:--append-system-prompt.description: Append a system prompt to the default system prompt (only works with --print)
Append a system prompt to the default system prompt

cli_flag:--append-system-prompt-file.description: Read system prompt from a file and append to the default system prompt

cli_flag:--ax-screen-reader.description: Render screen-reader friendly output (flat text, no decorative borders or animations).

cli_flag:--bare.description: Minimal mode: skip hooks, LSP, plugin sync, attribution, auto-memory, background prefetches, keychain reads, and CLAUDE.md auto-discovery. Sets CLAUDE_CODE_SIMPLE=1. Anthropic auth is strictly ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or apiKeyHelper via --settings (OAuth and keychain are never read). 3P providers (Bedrock/Vertex/Foundry) use their own credentials. Skills still resolve via /skill-name. Explicitly provide context via: --system-prompt[-file], --append-system-prompt[-file], --add-dir (CLAUDE.md dirs), --mcp-config, --settings, --agents, --plugin-dir.

cli_flag:--betas.description: Beta headers to include in API requests (API key users only)

cli_flag:--bg.description: Start the session as a background agent and return immediately (manage with `claude agents`)

cli_flag:--brief.description: Enable SendUserMessage tool for agent-to-user communication

cli_flag:--callback-port.description: Fixed port for OAuth callback (for servers requiring pre-registered redirect URIs)

cli_flag:--channels.description: MCP servers whose channel notifications (inbound push) should register this session. Space-separated server names.

cli_flag:--client-id.description: OAuth client ID for HTTP/SSE servers

cli_flag:--client-secret.description: Prompt for OAuth client secret (or set MCP_CLIENT_SECRET env var)

cli_flag:--cloud.description: Create a cloud session with the given description, or attach to an existing one by session ID or claude.ai/code URL

cli_flag:--config.description: Set a userConfig option declared in the plugin's manifest (repeatable). Values are validated against the schema and stored via the same path as the interactive /plugin configure flow.

cli_flag:--console.description: Use Anthropic Console (API usage billing) instead of Claude subscription

cli_flag:--continue.description: Continue the most recent conversation in the current directory

cli_flag:--cwd.description: Show only background sessions started under <path>

cli_flag:--dangerously-load-development-.description: Load channel servers not on the approved allowlist. For local channel development only. Shows a confirmation dialog at startup.

cli_flag:--dangerously-skip-permissions.description: Skip all permission checks. Only works in Docker containers with no internet access. Will crash otherwise.
Bypass all permission checks. Only works in Docker containers with no internet access.
Bypass all permission checks. Recommended only for sandboxes with no internet access.

cli_flag:--debug-file.description: Write debug logs to a specific file path (implicitly enables debug mode)

cli_flag:--debug-to-stderr.description: Redirect debug messages to stderr (requires --debug to be set)

cli_flag:--deep-link-cwd-b64.description: Base64url-encoded working directory (deep-link shell-safe launch paths)

cli_flag:--deep-link-last-fetch.description: FETCH_HEAD mtime in epoch ms, precomputed by the deep link trampoline

cli_flag:--deep-link-origin.description: Signal that this session was launched from a deep link

cli_flag:--deep-link-repo.description: Repo slug the deep link ?repo= parameter resolved to the current cwd

cli_flag:--disallowed.description: Comma or space-separated list of tool names to deny (e.g. "Bash(git*) Edit Write")
Comma or space-separated list of tool names to deny (e.g. "Bash(git:*) Edit Write")
Comma or space-separated list of tool names to deny (e.g. "Bash(git:*) Edit")
Comma or space-separated list of tool names to deny (e.g. "Bash(git *) Edit")

cli_flag:--dry-run.description: Print what would be tagged without creating it

cli_flag:--effort.description: Effort level for the current session (low, medium, high)
Effort level for the current session (low, medium, high, max)
Effort level for the current session (low, medium, high, xhigh, max)
Default effort level for sessions dispatched from agent view

cli_flag:--email.description: Pre-populate email address on the login page

cli_flag:--enable-auth-status.description: Enable auth status messages in SDK mode

cli_flag:--exclude-dynamic-system-prompt.description: Move per-machine sections (cwd, env info, memory paths, git status) from the system prompt into the first user message. Improves cross-user prompt-cache reuse. Only applies with the default system prompt (ignored with --system-prompt).

cli_flag:--fallback-model.description: Enable automatic fallback to specified model when default model is overloaded (only works with --print)
Enable automatic fallback to specified model when the default model is overloaded or not available (only works with --print)

Enable automatic fallback to specified model(s) when the default model is overloaded or not available. Accepts a comma-separated list to try each in order. Re-tries the primary at the start of each user turn. (only works with --print)

cli_flag:--file.description: File resources to download at startup. Format: file_id:relative_path (e.g., --file file_abc:doc.txt file_def:img.png)

cli_flag:--force.description: Force installation even if already installed

cli_flag:--fork-session.description: When resuming, create a new session ID instead of reusing the original (use with --resume or --continue)

cli_flag:--from-pr.description: Resume a session linked to a PR by PR number/URL, or open interactive picker with optional search term

cli_flag:--id-token.description: Write this pre-obtained id_token directly to cache, skipping the OIDC browser login

cli_flag:--ide.description: Automatically connect to IDE on startup if exactly one valid IDE is available

cli_flag:--idle-timeout.description: Idle timeout for detached sessions in ms (0 = never expire)

cli_flag:--include-hook-events.description: Include all hook lifecycle events in the output stream (only works with --output-format=stream-json)

cli_flag:--include-partial-messages.description: Include partial message chunks as they arrive (only works with --print and --output-format=stream-json)

cli_flag:--init.description: Run Setup hooks with init trigger, then continue

cli_flag:--init-only.description: Run Setup hooks with init trigger, then exit
Run Setup and SessionStart:startup hooks, then exit

cli_flag:--input-format.description: Input format (only works with --print): "text" (default), or "stream-json" (realtime streaming input)

cli_flag:--interactive.description: Prompt for each item before deleting

cli_flag:--json.description: Output JSON in the shape {cost_usd: number, duration_ms: number, duration_api_ms: number, result: string} (only works with --print)
[Deprecated: Use --output-format json instead] Output JSON in the shape {cost_usd: number, duration_ms: number, duration_api_ms: number, result: string} (only works with --print)

cli_flag:--json-schema.description: JSON Schema for structured output validation. Example: {"type":"object","properties":{"name":{"type":"string"}},"required":["name"]}

cli_flag:--keep-data.description: Preserve the plugin's persistent data directory (~/.claude/plugins/data/{id}/)

cli_flag:--maintenance.description: Run Setup hooks with maintenance trigger, then continue

cli_flag:--managed-settings.description: Policy-tier settings JSON from a spawning parent process (SDK use only)

cli_flag:--max-budget-usd.description: Maximum dollar amount to spend on API calls (only works with --print)

cli_flag:--max-cost-usd.description: Optional hard cost ceiling; abort and report partial results if hit (exit 2).

cli_flag:--max-thinking-tokens.description: Maximum number of thinking tokens. (only works with --print)
Maximum number of thinking tokens (only works with --print)
[DEPRECATED. Use --thinking instead for newer models] Maximum number of thinking tokens (only works with --print)

cli_flag:--max-turns.description: Maximum number of agentic turns in non-interactive mode. This will early exit the conversation after the specified number of turns. (only works with --print)

cli_flag:--mcp-config.description: Load MCP servers from a JSON file
Load MCP servers from a JSON file or string
Load MCP servers from JSON files or strings (space-separated)

cli_flag:--mcp-debug.description: Enable MCP debug mode (shows MCP server errors)
[DEPRECATED. Use --debug instead] Enable MCP debug mode (shows MCP server errors)

cli_flag:--message.description: Tag annotation message (use %s for the version)

cli_flag:--model.description: Model for the current session. Provide an alias for the latest model (e.g. 'sonnet' or 'opus') or a model's full name (e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-20250514').
Model for the current session. Provide an alias for the latest model (e.g. 'sonnet' or 'opus') or a model's full name (e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929').
Model for the current session. Provide an alias for the latest model (e.g. 'sonnet' or 'opus') or a model's full name (e.g. 'claude-sonnet-4-6').

cli_flag:--name.description: Set a display name for this session (shown in /resume and terminal title)
Set a display name for this session (shown in the prompt box, /resume picker, and terminal title)

cli_flag:--no-browser.description: Print the authorization URL instead of opening a browser (for SSH/headless sessions — paste the redirect URL back when prompted)

cli_flag:--no-session-persistence.description: Disable session persistence - sessions will not be saved to disk and cannot be resumed (only works with --print)

cli_flag:--output-format.description: Output format (only works with --print): "text" (default), "json" (single result), or "stream-json" (realtime streaming)

cli_flag:--parent-session-id.description: Parent session ID for analytics correlation

cli_flag:--permission-mode.description: Permission mode to use for the session (only works with --print)
Permission mode to use for the session

cli_flag:--permission-prompt-tool.description: MCP tool to use for permission prompts (only works with --print)

cli_flag:--plan-mode-instructions.description: Custom workflow body for plan mode. Replaces the default code-implementation phases in the plan-mode system reminder; the read-only enforcement preamble and ExitPlanMode protocol footer are always kept.

cli_flag:--plugin-dir.description: Load plugins from directories for this session only (repeatable)
Load plugins from a directory for this session only (repeatable: --plugin-dir A --plugin-dir B)
Load a plugin from a directory or .zip for this session only (repeatable: --plugin-dir A --plugin-dir B.zip)

cli_flag:--plugin-url.description: Fetch a plugin .zip from a URL for this session only (repeatable: --plugin-url A --plugin-url B)

cli_flag:--prefill.description: Pre-fill the prompt input with text without submitting it

cli_flag:--print.description: Print response and exit (useful for pipes)
Print response and exit (useful for pipes). Note: The workspace trust dialog is skipped when Claude is run with the -p mode. Only use this flag in directories you trust.

Print response and exit (useful for pipes). Note: The workspace trust dialog is skipped when Claude is run in non-interactive mode (via -p, or when stdout is not a TTY, e.g. piped or redirected output). Only use this in directories you trust.

Print response and exit (useful for pipes). Note: The workspace trust dialog is skipped when Claude is run in non-interactive mode (via -p, or when stdout is not a TTY, e.g. piped or redirected output). Only use this in directories you trust. Settings files that fail validation are silently ignored in this mode (no error dialog is shown).

cli_flag:--prompt-suggestions.description: Enable prompt suggestions. In print/SDK mode, emits a prompt_suggestion message after each turn with a predicted next user prompt

cli_flag:--prune.description: Also remove auto-installed dependencies that are no longer needed (requires -y in non-interactive contexts)

cli_flag:--push.description: Push the tag to --remote after creating it

cli_flag:--remote.description: Create a remote session with the given description
Create a remote session with the given description, or attach to an existing one by session ID or claude.ai/code URL

cli_flag:--remote-control.description: Start an interactive session with Remote Control enabled (optionally named)

cli_flag:--remote-control-session-name-p.description: Prefix for auto-generated Remote Control session names (default: hostname)

cli_flag:--replay-user-messages.description: Re-emit user messages from stdin back on stdout for acknowledgment (only works with --input-format=stream-json and --output-format=stream-json)

cli_flag:--reply-on-resume.description: When resuming, immediately query if the loaded transcript ends in a user-role message (set by /background mid-turn so the fork continues the in-flight turn).

cli_flag:--resume.description: Show conversation picker to select a conversation to resume
Resume a conversation - provide a session ID or interactively select a conversation to resume
Resume a conversation by session ID, or open interactive picker with optional search term

cli_flag:--resume-session-at.description: When resuming, only messages up to and including the assistant message with <message.id> (use with --resume in print mode)

cli_flag:--rewind-files.description: Restore files to state at the specified user message and exit (requires --resume)

cli_flag:--safe-mode.description: Start with all customizations (CLAUDE.md, skills, plugins, hooks, MCP servers, custom commands and agents, output styles, workflows, custom themes, keybindings, and more) disabled — useful for troubleshooting a broken configuration. Admin-managed (policy) settings still apply. Auth, model selection, built-in tools, and permissions work normally. Sets CLAUDE_CODE_SAFE_MODE=1.

cli_flag:--scaffold.description: Run each case's scaffold_script (runs author-supplied bash as you; off by default — only use on case files you authored)

cli_flag:--sdk-url.description: Use remote WebSocket endpoint for SDK I/O streaming (only with -p and stream-json format)

cli_flag:--session-id.description: Use a specific session ID for the conversation (must be a valid UUID)
Use a specific session ID for the conversation (UUID or tagged ID)
Use a specific session ID for the conversation (must be a valid UUID)

cli_flag:--session-mirror.description: Emit transcript_mirror frames on stdout (SDK-internal; set by ProcessTransport when sessionStore is configured)

cli_flag:--setting-sources.description: Comma-separated list of setting sources to load (user, project, local).

cli_flag:--settings.description: Path to a settings JSON file to load additional settings from
Path to a settings JSON file or a JSON string to load additional settings from

cli_flag:--sparse.description: Limit checkout to specific directories via git sparse-checkout (for monorepos). Example: --sparse .claude-plugin plugins

cli_flag:--strict.description: Treat warnings as errors (exit 1). Use in CI to fail on unrecognized fields, missing metadata, and other issues that the runtime tolerates.

cli_flag:--strict-mcp-config.description: Only use MCP servers from --mcp-config, ignoring all other MCP configurations

cli_flag:--system-prompt.description: Override the system prompt (only works with --print)
System prompt to use for the session (only works with --print)
System prompt to use for the session

cli_flag:--system-prompt-file.description: Read system prompt from a file (only works with --print)
Read system prompt from a file

cli_flag:--teleport.description: Resume a teleport session, optionally specify session ID

cli_flag:--thinking.description: Thinking mode: enabled (equivalent to adaptive), disabled

cli_flag:--thinking-display.description: How thinking content appears in the response

cli_flag:--threshold.description: Exit 1 if any case score is below this threshold (default: 1.0)

cli_flag:--timeout.description: Timeout in milliseconds (default: MCP_TOOL_TIMEOUT env var or effectively infinite)
Maximum minutes to wait for the review to finish (default: 30)

cli_flag:--tmux.description: Create a tmux session for the worktree (requires --worktree). Uses iTerm2 native panes when available; use --tmux=classic for traditional tmux.

cli_flag:--tools.description: Specify the list of available tools from the built-in set. Use "" to disable all tools, "default" to use all tools, or specify tool names (e.g. "Bash,Edit,Read"). Only works with --print mode.
Specify the list of available tools from the built-in set. Use "" to disable all tools, "default" to use all tools, or specify tool names (e.g. "Bash,Edit,Read").

cli_flag:--transport.description: Transport type (stdio, sse, http). Defaults to stdio if not specified.

cli_flag:--unix.description: Listen on a unix domain socket

cli_flag:--verbose.description: Override verbose mode setting from config

cli_flag:--workload.description: Workload tag for billing-header attribution (cc_workload). Process-scoped; set by SDK daemon callers that spawn subprocesses for cron work. (only works with --print)

cli_flag:--workspace.description: Default working directory for sessions that do not specify cwd

cli_flag:--worktree.description: Create a new git worktree for this session (optionally specify a name)

cli_flag:--xaa.description: Enable XAA (SEP-990) for this server. Requires 'claude mcp xaa setup' first. Also requires --client-id and --client-secret (for the MCP server's AS).
